data.title: Maldives fully settles $500 million sukuk to reduce debt burden
data.description: The government of the Maldives said on Thursday it has fully settled its $500 million sukuk, an Islamic finance instrument equivalent to a bond, in a move aimed at reducing its debt stock and improving debt sustainability.

data.title: Canada's Goods-Trade Deficit Widens to Six-Month High Even as Trade With Non-U.S. Nations Increases — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart

OTTAWA--Canada's trade deficit with the world surged to a six-month high as shipments of gold drove imports to record levels, though evidence is growing the country's dependence on the massive U.S. market is easing.

The country recorded a merchandise-trade deficit in February of 5.74 billion Canadian dollars, the equivalent of about US$4.14 billion, Statistics Canada said Thursday. That was much larger than the C$2.5 billion shortfall economists anticipated.

Imports jumped 8.4% between January and February to an all-time high of C$72.05 billion as inbound shipments of gold led widespread increases, outpacing a 6.4% rise in exports to C$66.31 billion, the highest since March of last year when the first wave of the Trump administration's tariffs kicked in.

Trade has been more volatile than usual for more than a year following the U.S. shift to more protectionist policies. Canada has racked up trade deficits five months running, and the C$9.92 billion gap in the first two months of 2026 marked a record year-to-date shortfall.

Although exports were up strongly in the latest month, climbing 5.2% in volume terms from a month earlier, that paled against a 9.4% surge in the volume of imports. Even if the balance flips in March, economists expect net trade will be a headwind to growth in the first quarter for an economy already showing strains in its labor market and a slowdown in housing activity.

Canada's cross border trade with the U.S., by far the country's biggest market, picked up in February. As did two-way trade with countries beyond America, with both exports to and imports from non-U.S. nations hitting records for the month. The result was Canada's longstanding goods-trade surplus with its neighbor continuing to narrow, hitting its smallest since May 2020 at C$1.73 billion, even as the deficit with all other countries narrowed to C$7.48 billion.

Former central banker Mark Carney was elected to office last April as a counter to President Trump, with pledges to decouple Canada's dependence on the U.s. and double non-U.S. exports in the next decade. The prime minister and government officials have made a series of trips to China, India, Europe and elsewhere in an effort to find new markets for the country's energy, minerals and other goods. Data earlier this week from the national statistics agency showed exports of crude oil to countries other than the U.S. hit a fresh record in January and shipments of natural gas were the highest since the country began exporting from a terminal on the west coast in mid-2025.

Canada's economy is on track to show modest growth in the first quarter of the year, before the full shock of higher oil prices due to the Iran war is felt. Gross domestic product edged up 0.1% in January from a month earlier as growth in oil and gas extraction and in construction helped to counter a pullback in manufacturing, and advance data from Statistics Canada indicated the pace accelerated to 0.2% growth in February as manufacturing recovered.

Exchanges of gold played a big part in the swings in trade in February. Excluding precious metals and their alloys, which for Canada is largely unwrought gold, exports for the month were up 5.5% and imports rose 5.8%.

Imports were up across most categories of goods, albeit led by record imports of gold with a jump in purchases from the U.S. Coinciding with a rebound in Canadian auto production following extended winter shutdowns at Ontario assembly plants, imports of motor vehicles and parts were up strongly. Canada's auto-manufacturing industry is deeply integrated with the U.S., but has felt the pressure of tariffs and trade policy uncertainty.

Canadian exports of motor vehicles and parts soared 24.2% in February following a slump the month before. Precious-metals shipments from Canada also were up strongly, led by a rise in gold exports to the U.K. Exports of unwrought aluminum recovered from a drop at the start of the year, and there was a rise in agricultural shipments to China following an agreement in January that saw China lower most tariffs on Canadian canola in exchange for allowing a limited number of Chinese electric vehicles to be brought into Canada.

"Despite a wider deficit and plenty of noise in both autos and gold, the broad-based increase in exports and imports is a positive for economic activity," said Shelly Kaushik, senior economist at Bank of Montreal Capital Markets. "While the China deal highlights some upside from efforts toward market diversification, overall activity will remain under pressure as long as the Canada-U.S. relationship is subject to elevated uncertainty.

Looking ahead, economists don't expect to see a similar jump in gold imports in March while exports will reflect the spike in crude oil prices, even as energy costs for consumers rise.

Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com

data.title: Dubai's Emirates NBD gets India's central bank nod to become majority owner of RBL Bank
data.description:
By Ashwin Manikandan and Nishit Navin

India's central bank has approved Emirates NBD Bank's proposal to acquire a majority stake in RBL Bank , the Mumbai-based lender said on Thursday, giving a key regulatory clearance for one of the largest cross-border deals in India's financial sector.

The Reserve Bank of India approved Emirates NBD to acquire up to 74% of RBL Bank's share capital, requiring it to maintain at least 51% ownership. RBI capped Emirates NBD's voting rights at 26% of RBL Bank's total voting rights.

In October last year, Emirates NBD announced plans to buy a 60% stake in the Indian lender for $3 billion.

The bank will invest 268.53 billion Indian rupees ($3.05 billion) in the Indian lender through a preferential issue of shares and will have the right to nominate directors to the board, RBL Bank had said.

RBL Bank will be classified as a foreign bank subsidiary with Emirates NBD as its parent, the RBI said. It will be governed by norms for wholly owned foreign subsidiaries, but exempt from the requirement that at least half of attending board members be independent directors.

India's competition regulator cleared the deal in January. The RBI approval is valid for one year.

Dealmaking in India's financial services sector has gained momentum over the past year. The approval comes as Gulf-based businesses face disruptions due to the ongoing war in the region.

As of December 2025, RBL Bank had assets of 1.57 trillion rupees ($17.08 billion) and deposits worth 1.19 trillion rupees ($12.95 billion), according to an investor presentation for the quarter ended December.

data.title: ICE Canola Rising Ahead of Long Weekend
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange were higher mid-session Thursday, recovering most of Wednesday's losses.

U.S. President Trump said in a televised address Wednesday that Iran will be hit hard by attacks over the next two to three weeks while stopping short of saying when he expected the war would end. As a result, crude oil prices climbed $5 to $9 per barrel.

An analyst said rising crude oil was spilling over into Chicago soyoil and, in turn, influencing canola. Chicago soyoil gained more than one U.S. cent per pound, while European rapeseed and Malaysian palm oil were higher.

The Canadian dollar was down more than one-tenth of a U.S. cent compared with Wednesday's close.

There will be no trading on Friday due to the Good Friday holiday.

About 31,100 canola contracts have traded at 10:02 CDT.

Prices in Canadian dollars per metric ton:

 
           Price      Change 
May       726.80     up 8.30 
Jul       740.20     up 8.40 
Nov       733.50     up 8.00 
Jan       739.60     up 8.20

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com

data.title: Blue Owl stokes more private-credit worries, as it paid out less than a quarter of requests
data.description:
By Tomi Kilgore

Blue Owl Credit Income said it received redemption requests for 21.9% of shares outstanding, but kept redemptions at just 5%

Blue Owl's stock falls after its private-credit fund capped redemptions at just 23% of what was requested.

Shares of Blue Owl Capital dipped into record-low territory Thursday, after the private-credit fund of the asset manager said it restricted its first-quarter payout to investors to less than a quarter of what investors wanted.

The "OCIC" fund - Blue Owl Credit Income Corp. - tried to assure investors that it had "ample liquidity" to meet its tender obligations and future offers. With OCIC headlining the long list of funds that have denied redemption requests, investor fears - not only about the health of Blue Owl's fund, but about the private-credit market in general - are back in the spotlight.

Also read: The chorus of disapproval for private credit gets louder as Morgan Stanley fund is the latest to cap withdrawals.

Blue Owl's stock (OWL) fell 1.3% in recent morning trading. It pared earlier losses of as much as 8.7% to an intraday low of $7.95, which was below the current record closing low of $8.56 hit on Oct. 13, 2022. The stock has tumbled 42.4% so far this year, and 59.4% over the past 12 months.

That selloff is hurting shares of firms with private-credit exposure, as Apollo Global Management's stock (APO) dropped 2.8%; Ares Management shares (ARES) slid 2%; Blackstone shares (BX) lost 1.2%; and Carlyle Group's stock (CG) gave up 1.7%. Meanwhile, KKR's stock (KKR) inched up 0.1%, but had been down as much as 3.6% at its intraday low.

OCIC disclosed Thursday that, during the first quarter, it received redemption requests of 21.9% of the shares outstanding as of Dec. 31. The fund said, however, that it was keeping to its terms of capping redemptions at 5% of the shares outstanding per quarter, which represents a little less than 23% of the requests.

In a letter to shareholders, Craig Packer, the head of credit and co-president of Blue Owl Capital, acknowledged investor concerns.

"We continue to observe a meaningful disconnect between the public dialogue on private credit and the underlying trends in our portfolio," Packer wrote.

The fund "is in a strong position to meet its current tender obligation and any future tender offers," he wrote, as it had liquidity of $11.3 billion. He said the 5% tender offer for the first quarter represented an outflow of $988 million - but inflows were $872 million. The $116 million difference was less than 1% of OCIC's net asset value.

Packer also noted that OCIC has delivered a 9.2% annualized return since inception, and that the debt-to-equity ratio of 0.80, a measure of net leverage, was below its target range of 0.90 to 1.25.

Basically, Packer's point was that the high redemption requests are driven more by investor fear about private credit than reality.

"We believe elevated tender activity was driven by market perception, as negative sentiment toward the asset class, intensified after peers reported tender results," OCIC said.

Read: These private-credit funds are giving back less than half the money their investors want.

-Tomi Kilgore

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Wall St pares losses as investors anticipate reopening of Strait of Hormuz
data.description:
Key points:
  • Indexes: Dow flat, S&P 500 up 0.8%, Nasdaq up 0.9%
  • Asset managers drop after Blue Owl caps investor withdrawal
  • Airlines shares tumble on rising crude prices
  • Indexes set for biggest weekly jump in four months

By Purvi Agarwal and Twesha Dikshit

Wall Street's main indexes pared declines and were muted on Thursday, in the last session of a holiday-truncated week, as investors assessed latest indications that energy shipping through the crucial Strait of Hormuz could be restored.

Iran was drafting a protocol with Oman for traffic through the Strait, its foreign ministry said. Britain also said that about 40 countries are discussing joint action to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to stop Iran from holding "the global economy hostage".

"It looks like the UK may be leading an effort to open the Strait of Hormuz, which would be great for traders," said Kim Forrest, chief investment officer at Bokeh Capital Partners.

"Not only does it benefit all people on the Arabian Peninsula, but Iran needs to get its products to market too. That probably is softening today's sell off."

Wall Street's indexes had opened sharply lower on Thursday after President Donald Trump signaled more aggressive attacks on Iran, in a sharp reversal from his earlier comments that the U.S. will be "out of Iran pretty quickly".

Oil prices pared some gains, but the elevated prices pressured airlines. United Airlines , Delta Airlines (DAL.O) and American Airlines lost between 1% and 3%.

The S&P 500 energy index added 0.4%.

Separately, private credit jitters resurfaced after Blue Owl capped the amount investors can withdraw from two of its retail-focused funds, sending its shares down marginally.

Other asset managers, including Apollo Global , Blackstone and Ares Management , also inched lower.

At 11:13 a.m. ET, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 12.26 points, or 0.03%, to 46,553.48, the S&P 500 gained 5.81 points, or 0.09%, to 6,581.11 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 17.21 points, or 0.08%, to 21,858.15.

Friday's nonfarm payroll numbers will be in the spotlight after weekly jobless claims fell last week, but U.S. markets will remain closed for the Good Friday holiday.

Investor will also focus on developments around Elon Musk's SpaceX, which confidentially filed for a U.S. initial public offering on Wednesday, and is expected to target a $1.75 trillion valuation.

Globalstar's shares jumped 9.5% after a report said Amazon is in talks to buy the low-earth-orbit communication satellites company.

Advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 1.09-to-1 ratio on the NYSE and a 1.03-to-1 ratio on the Nasdaq.

The S&P 500 posted 8 new 52-week highs and 12 new lows, while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 41 new highs and 109 new lows.

data.title: Redwire Stock Gains on European Space Agency Satellite Contract
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Shares of Redwire rose after the company received a contract to build a quantum-secure satellite for the European Space Agency.

Shares were up 7.9% at $9.80 in late morning trading. The stock is up 30% year-to-date.

The aerospace and defense technology company said Thursday that it will deliver a European-built Hammerhead spacecraft for the ESA's Quantum Key Distribution Satellite, part of the agency's Advanced Research in Telecommunications Systems Partnership Projects program.

"Quantum secure communications are critical to the future of European autonomy," said Executive Vice President of Redwire Europe Marc Dielissen. "We are proud to leverage Redwire's expertise in spacecraft development and avionics to support QKDSat."

Redwire is part of a consortium working on QKDSat led by Honeywell Aerospace, and including Craft Prospect, BT Group, and QTLabs.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Energy subsidies to cost Morocco $180 million a month, minister says
data.description: Morocco expects to spend up to 1.6 billion dirhams ($180 million) a month subsidising butane cooking gas, electricity and public transport operators to keep prices stable amid a surge in international energy markets, budget minister Fouzi Lekjaa said on Thursday.

data.title: European Q1 corporate profits expected to grow 4% helped by booming energy sector
data.description:
European blue chip companies are set to deliver growing profits in the first quarter, the latest forecasts showed on Thursday, thanks to strong expectations for energy companies.

STOXX 600 companies are expected to report growth of 4% in first-quarter earnings, on average, according to LSEG I/B/E/S data, compared to the 2% year-on-year decline the previous quarter.

  • That is mostly due to a windfall blessing for energy companies as the ongoing war in the Middle East has raised crude prices by 50% to 70%

  • Earnings of energy companies are expected to rise by 24.9% according to LSEG's report, while other sectors are set to deliver a 1.5% increase on average

  • Revenues are also seen growing compared to the same period last year, albeit at a slower rate —compared to earnings— of 1.7%, the data showed

  • Revenues have lagged earnings in seven of the past eight quarters, showing that companies efforts to cut costs and restructure businesses could be paying off

  • Estimates for European companies' earnings have considerably improved in the past months, especially after the conflict began

  • Profits of European majors were expected to grow at a very modest 0.9% at the beginning of the year, but estimates have consistently improved in recent weeks

  • Investors say the reverberations of the conflict will take a while to feed through the economy

data.title: US considering permanent ban on air bags components produced by Chinese manufacturer DTN
data.description:
By David Shepardson

The U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said on Thursday it is considering a permanent ban on substandard Chinese replacement automobile air bag inflators produced by DTN after 10 fatal crashes since May 2023.

The auto safety agency said it was aware of 12 crashes in the U.S. resulting in deaths or serious injury from ruptured replacement air bag inflators made in China by Jilin Province Detiannuo Automobile Safety System Co Ltd, also known as DTN, and illegally imported into the U.S., since May 2023. All of the deaths have taken place in GM or Hyundai vehicles.

NHTSA said it made an initial determination that the air bag inflators pose an unreasonable risk to safety. NHTSA said the DTN air bag inflators malfunctioned in otherwise survivable crashes, "sending large metal fragments into drivers’ chests, necks, eyes and faces."

The agency issued an urgent warning to owners and repair shops in January.

NHTSA said the most recent death occurred Feb. 16 in Clarksdale, Mississippi, in a 2020 Chevrolet Malibu.

The agency will seek public comment and provide DTN with the opportunity to present information challenging the decision. NHTSA is still investigating how many of these inflators have illegally entered the U.S.

NHTSA in October opened an investigation into DTN replacement inflators. NHTSA is working with law enforcement agencies to address any illegal activities associated with the importation of these inflators and previously estimated the population of inflators under investigation at 10,000.

Nine of the replacement air bag inflators in the fatal crashes were installed in used GM Chevrolet Malibu vehicles and four in Hyundai , but NHTSA said it could not confirm the risk is limited to these makes and models.

DTN says on its website that the inflators are prohibited from sale in the U.S.

"Whoever is bringing them into the country and installing them is putting American families in danger," NHTSA said.

For consumers buying used vehicles that were in a previous crash where the air bag deployed, the air bag should be inspected immediately to ensure that it is a legitimate replacement equivalent to the original, NHTSA said.

data.title: DexTools to Host World Mobile Livestream on April 3
data.description:
DEXTools announced that it will go live with World Mobile on April 3 at 18:00 UTC. According to the post, the stream will focus on World Mobile, which the company describes as one of the fastest-growing decentralized mobile networks. The event will also include a $1,000 giveaway and 20 AirNodes.

Refer to the official tweet by DEXT:

DEXTools
@DEXToolsApp

This Friday weâ€™re going live with @WorldMobileTeam building one of the fastest growing decentralized mobile networks! 🌍📡

🗓 April 3 | 6PM UTC⁰🎁 $1000 giveaway 20 x AirNodes

See you there! pic.twitter.com/tAZX4CdtVN

Apr 02, 2026

DEXT Info

DEXTools is a comprehensive application designed for traders that consolidates information from decentralized markets into a unified user interface. The platform facilitates a clearer understanding of trading activities, investments, and the overall status of the cryptocurrency market.

The DEXTools ecosystem offers a range of functionalities, including the Pool Explorer for monitoring liquidity movements and new pool formations on decentralized exchanges. The Pair Explorer provides insights into token charts, transactions, trust, and other project metrics. Additionally, users can track specific trader’s wallets using Wallet info, monitor large transactions through Big Swap Explorer, and utilize Multiswap to access multiple DEX portals, enhancing trading efficiency.

DEXT, the native utility token of the platform, offers users access to advanced features of the app, either by holding the token or through a monthly subscription. Furthermore, DEXT allows entry to premium trading groups, community initiatives, and exclusive ventures like DEXT Force Ventures.

data.title: Ford Motor Company US Q1 Total Sales Decline 8.8%
data.description:
DEARBORN (dpa-AFX) - Ford Motor Company (F), Thursday announced that the total sales for the first quarter declined 8.8 percent, while delivering a higher first quarter retail share amid a shifting industry landscape.

Ford noted that demand for F-Series truck, and large SUVs like Bronco, Explorer and Expedition climbed during the period.

'The first quarter showed our team worked hard to maintain retail share and navigate a changing industry, while underlying demand remains strong for F-Series, SUVs and the Ford Pro business,' said Andrew Frick, president, Ford Blue and Model e.

'We're focusing on our high-demand segment strengths while continuing to meet the needs of our customers with affordable and premium vehicle choices.'

Currently, F is trading at $11.48, down 1.67 percent on the New York Stock Exchange.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Tesla's Underwhelming 1Q Deliveries Weren't A Surprise For Wedbush — Market Talk
data.description: Wedbush says it wasn't surprised that Tesla missed Wall Street's 1Q delivery expectations, "given the current EV backdrop across geographies while the company shifts gears to focus more on its AI strategy." The analysts say in a research note that they're looking forward to hearing more on the earnings call near the end of the month. They emphasize that investors are focused on robotaxi and full self-driving updates. "All eyes remain on the company's AI rollout, including the company's switch in long-term mission of developing a sustainable revenue stream from AI and robotics," they say. Tesla falls 3.8%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)
